claim: AI agents are programs where large language model outputs control the workflow, involving multiple LLM calls, tool usage, a planner, and autonomous decision-making
actionable: Design your agentic system with LLM calls, tools, a coordinator, and guard rails to enable autonomous decision-making rather than predefined paths
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Wrapped in agentic frameworks, LLMs show dramatically improved performance: humanity's last exam went from ~10% accuracy (single LLM) to 25%+ accuracy when using chain-of-thought reasoning with agents
actionable: Use agentic workflows with step-by-step chain of thought processing and real-time verification rather than single LLM calls for complex reasoning tasks
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: The OpenAI Agents SDK is lightweight, simple, and handles tool calling complexity automatically - unlike the ChatCompletions API which requires manual JSON configuration
actionable: Use OpenAI Agents SDK instead of raw ChatCompletions API to avoid JSON boilerplate and simplify tool integration
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Structured outputs with Pydantic objects improve LLM reasoning by forcing it to output reasoning before final answers - even if you don't use the reasoning field
actionable: Add a 'reasoning' or 'explanation' field to your Pydantic output schema before result fields to bias the model toward better chain-of-thought
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Deep research agents that search web, retrieve information, and synthesize reports show massive capability improvements over single-step LLM outputs
actionable: Build multi-step agentic workflows with research agents that search, aggregate, and synthesize data to improve answer quality
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Use maximum execution limits (default 10 turns for OpenAI SDK, adjustable to 30 for longer tasks) and API-level spend caps to prevent runaway costs in agentic systems
actionable: Set max_iterations in your agent config and enforce spending limits in your API account to prevent expensive loops
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Cheaper models like GPT-4o mini reduce financial stakes for agentic experiments while avoiding situations where agents run dollars-per-second with expensive models like o1
actionable: Start with GPT-4o mini for agent prototyping and testing, only graduate to expensive models like o1 once behavior is validated
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Crew AI is more opinionated than OpenAI Agents SDK (with batteries included) but harder to debug; use Crew for rapid prototyping with configurations, OpenAI SDK for fine-grained control
actionable: Choose Crew AI when you want fast setup with YAML configuration and built-in features; use OpenAI Agents SDK when you need lower-level control and debugging visibility
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a standard for connecting agents to tools/data sources, not an agent framework itself - it's the USBC-equivalent for standardized tool integration
actionable: Use MCP to integrate multiple tools with a common protocol rather than writing custom glue code for each tool
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: MCP servers typically run locally (via pip install or similar) not remotely - they may call remote APIs, but the server itself is local
actionable: When implementing MCP servers, run them locally on your machine rather than assuming they must be remote infrastructure
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Five workflow design patterns exist for predefined agentic systems: prompt chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator-worker, and evaluator-optimizer
actionable: Choose between these five patterns based on your control needs: use prompt chaining for simple sequences, routing for conditional branching, parallelization for concurrent subtasks, orchestrator for dynamic coordination, evaluator for quality gates
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Evaluator-optimizer workflows where a second LLM validates outputs can multiply accuracy: 0.99 * 0.99 = 0.9801 effective accuracy with two 99%-accurate models
actionable: Add a validation agent that checks outputs from your generator agent to dramatically reduce hallucination rates
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Crew AI with allow_code_execution=true and code_execution_mode='safe' handles Docker container setup automatically for safe agent code execution
actionable: Enable code execution in Crew AI with just two parameters; the framework handles Docker container management automatically
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: A 'coder agent' in Crew AI means any agent that can write Python code to achieve its goal (not just agents that output code)
actionable: Enable code execution on agents that need to compute answers, not just code-generating agents - e.g., a support agent might use Python to find the 50th decimal of pi
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Crew AI enables agents to work together sequentially or hierarchically - hierarchical mode uses a manager LLM to decide task ordering
actionable: Use sequential mode for linear task flows, hierarchical mode when you want an LLM manager to dynamically order tasks
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: MCP clients run inside a host application (Claude desktop, Cursor, or agent architecture) and have one-to-one mappings with MCP servers
actionable: For each MCP server you want to use, configure a corresponding MCP client inside your host application
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Starting small with narrowly-defined agentic solutions is critical for adoption - find one clear business process, measure improvement, then expand gradually
actionable: Begin with a single well-defined task (e.g., automating one customer support category), demonstrate ROI, then expand to larger multi-agent systems
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Working with LLMs requires a science/data science mindset first, software engineering mindset second - experimentation and measurement are more important than code architecture
actionable: Treat your agentic system development as an experiment: measure performance, iterate on prompts, test thoroughly before production
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Multi-agent agentic systems are fundamentally unpredictable - test with traces and monitoring to understand behavior, don't assume deterministic execution
actionable: Always inspect agent traces and monitor outputs - expect variability between runs and design guardrails accordingly
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK

claim: Guardrails are critical for production agentic systems - they ensure agents behave safely, consistently, and within intended boundaries
actionable: Implement guardrails at the framework level (built into OpenAI Agents SDK, Crew) and application level to constrain agent behavior
source_title: Agentic AI Engineering: Complete 4-Hour Workshop feat. MCP, CrewAI and OpenAI Agents SDK
